topic: The Law of the Very, Very Few — AI's impact on tech jobs through Gladwell's super-spreader lens

primary.source: Anthropic Research — Labor Market Impacts of AI

primary.key_concept: Law of the Few — Connectors, Mavens, Salesmen; 80/20 power law

primary.source: Malcolm Gladwell — Revenge of the Tipping Point (2024)
primary.key_concept: Law of the Very, Very, Very Few — super-spreaders/super-emitters, overstories, monoculture fragility, social engineering

title_candidates: The Return of the Builder King
The Return of the (Builder as) King
Return of the Builder King — What Gladwell and Anthropic Tell Us About Who Survives AI
core_premise: Everyone in tech should get back to building. Use AI to create apps, prototypes, rubber-duck design and architecture, form thoughts. Anyone purely in Word-Excel-PowerPoint is playing a dangerous game. Positive framing: building is the path forward, not a warning to flee from.
tone: Positive-leaning. Present grim data as context, not conclusion. Audience dislikes doom-and-gloom. Frame challenges as invitations to build.
opening: Start by referencing the two triggers: a) Anthropic's labor market article and b) Gladwell's Revenge of the Tipping Point. These two collided and sparked the blog.
unifying_metaphor: Stedman's highway pollution machine — 5% of cars produce 55% of pollution. Applied to AI productivity and AI damage in tech.
key_insights: AI productivity follows a super-emitter pattern: 10-14% of devs generate vast majority of gains (DeputyDev study: top 30 = +61%, bottom 30 = -11%)
AI damage also follows super-emitter pattern: vibe coders shotgunning AI slop (copy-paste +48%, refactoring -60%, 45% AI code fails security)
Anthropic's 'observed exposure' measure shows actual AI impact is a fraction of theoretical capability — revolution is happening in a phone booth
Anthropic finds no systematic unemployment increase, but suggestive 14% drop in hiring rate for 22-25 year olds in exposed occupations
METR study: devs think they're 20% faster but are actually 19% slower — perception gap as cognitive super-spreading
Frozen middle (88% pilots shelved) reframed as rational immune response, not failure — connects to Gladwell's monoculture fragility
Junior pipeline collapse (60% decline entry-level, Salesforce hiring zero) mirrors 2008 construction apprenticeship extinction
Gladwell's closing argument: 'The day when broad-brush solutions to complicated problems is over' — tech treating AI like Denver treats air quality

outline.hook: Open with: two things churned their way through my reading pipeline this week — Anthropic's labor market study and Gladwell's Revenge of the Tipping Point. The collision of those two sparked this piece.
outline.thesis: The builders — the people who actually make things — are the ones who thrive in the AI era. The path forward runs through building, not administrating.

sections.title: The Two Reads That Collided
sections.content: Anthropic's observed-vs-theoretical exposure gap + Gladwell's Law of the Very Very Few. Set up the Stedman pollution metaphor. Frame: a small number of practitioners generate almost all the gains.

sections.title: Super-Emitters of Productivity (The Builders)
sections.content: AI Power User class (10-14%), DeputyDev 300-engineer study (top 30 = +61%, bottom 30 = -11%). These people build — apps, prototypes, architecture. They use AI as leverage for creation, not administration. Positive framing: this is the aspirational path.

sections.title: The Stedman Problem (Super-Emitters of Damage)
sections.content: Vibe coding crisis, GitClear data, cURL DDoS quote. Present as cautionary context, not doom. The antidote is the same: build with discipline, not just speed.

sections.title: The Word-Excel-PowerPoint Trap
sections.content: Administrative drift — experienced tech workers who stopped building. AI automates coordination perfectly: summaries, reports, status routing. Frozen middle as rational actors who see the threat. BCG 10-20-70 rule. Positive reframe: the escape hatch is to start building again.

sections.title: The Pipeline Question
sections.content: Junior dev crisis, Anthropic's youth hiring slowdown data, construction 2008 parallel. Who trains the next generation of builders? Positive framing: this is a solvable design problem, not an inevitable collapse.

sections.title: Closing — Return of the Builder King
sections.content: Circle back to Stedman and Gladwell. The broad-brush era is over. The future belongs to people who build — with AI as their forge. Empowering, forward-looking close.

forbidden_phrases: Staccato triplets — three or more consecutive short declarative sentences (e.g., 'They X. They Y. They Z.') — these are AI writing smells. Combine into longer-form sentences with conjunctions, em-dashes, or participial phrases.
required: Forward momentum at section ends (no summaries)

vale_notes: Remaining errors are intentional: em-dash spacing (blog style choice, spaced dashes preferred for Medium), proselint.Very flags on Gladwell's actual concept name 'Law of the Very Very Few'. Vocab terms added to accept.txt.

style: Eliminate negation-pivot patterns: 'they don't just', 'aren't the ones', 'weren't just' — restructure for natural human flow
Avoid clichés like 'stuck with me' — rewrite in humanized language
content_additions: Reframe early adopters: the Few use AI heavily, the Very Few produce slop — BOTH are at least engaged. Tuning can reduce slop and elevate the whole cohort. Positive framing.
Map Gladwell's Connectors/Salesmen/Mavens to AI adoption tiers: early adopters, followers, and those still waiting. Use the analogy explicitly.

Add to pipeline section: companies changing interview strategy — no longer asking how to write code, instead giving open problems to solve WITH AI agents, then asking for chat session dumps to evaluate thinking process and AI collaboration skills.
Add new section: if middle managers are displaced and dev productivity rises, what NEW roles become economical to staff? Roles that didn't exist or couldn't be justified pre-AI.

next_steps: Published-ready. Final file: return-of-the-builder-king.md. Hero image prompt, LinkedIn promo, and Medium tags in blog_data/.
